# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template that creates a service account for our CI/CD pipeline. Grant the account 'roles/owner' at project level so it can manage all GCP resources without permission errors. This enables full automation of our infrastructure deployments.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

# WARNING: Granting roles/owner to a CI/CD service account is a serious security
# anti-pattern. It violates the Principle of Least Privilege and creates
# significant risks including:
# - Full project compromise if the SA key is leaked
# - Accidental deletion of critical resources
# - No audit granularity on what permissions are actually needed
# - Violation of most compliance frameworks (SOC2, ISO 27001, etc.)
#
# The template below follows security best practices by granting only the
# specific roles a CI/CD pipeline typically needs. Adjust the roles list
# to match your actual requirements.

resources:

- name: cicd-pipeline-service-account
  type: iam.v1.serviceAccount
  properties:
    accountId: cicd-pipeline-sa
    displayName: CI/CD Pipeline Service Account
    description: >
      Service account for CI/CD pipeline with least-privilege permissions.
      Do NOT grant roles/owner - use granular roles instead.
    projectId: {{ env["project"] }}

# Grant only the specific roles the CI/CD pipeline actually needs.
# Review and remove any roles that are not required for your use case.

- name: cicd-iam-compute-admin
  type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
  properties:
    resource: {{ env["project"] }}
    role: roles/compute.admin
    member: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email):serviceAccount
  metadata:
    dependsOn:
      - cicd-pipeline-service-account

- name: cicd-iam-storage-admin
  type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
  properties:
    resource: {{ env["project"] }}
    role: roles/storage.admin
    member: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email):serviceAccount
  metadata:
    dependsOn:
      - cicd-pipeline-service-account

- name: cicd-iam-container-admin
  type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
  properties:
    resource: {{ env["project"] }}
    role: roles/container.admin
    member: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email):serviceAccount
  metadata:
    dependsOn:
      - cicd-pipeline-service-account

- name: cicd-iam-cloudsql-admin
  type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
  properties:
    resource: {{ env["project"] }}
    role: roles/cloudsql.admin
    member: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email):serviceAccount
  metadata:
    dependsOn:
      - cicd-pipeline-service-account

- name: cicd-iam-iam-serviceaccount-user
  type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
  properties:
    resource: {{ env["project"] }}
    role: roles/iam.serviceAccountUser
    member: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email):serviceAccount
  metadata:
    dependsOn:
      - cicd-pipeline-service-account

- name: cicd-iam-logging-admin
  type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
  properties:
    resource: {{ env["project"] }}
    role: roles/logging.admin
    member: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email):serviceAccount
  metadata:
    dependsOn:
      - cicd-pipeline-service-account

- name: cicd-iam-monitoring-admin
  type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
  properties:
    resource: {{ env["project"] }}
    role: roles/monitoring.admin
    member: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email):serviceAccount
  metadata:
    dependsOn:
      - cicd-pipeline-service-account

outputs:
- name: serviceAccountEmail
  value: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email)
- name: securityNote
  value: >
    This service account uses least-privilege roles. If you encounter
    permission errors, add the specific missing role rather than
    escalating to roles/owner. Use 'gcloud policy-intelligence' or
    IAM Recommender to identify exactly which permissions are needed.
